steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The claim specifies that Winnemucca creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event. The signal is physical sensations or bodily reactions — heart racing, trembling, breathing — used to convey tension or fear about something uncertain or threatening.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly expresses suspense through physical sensation: her heart jumps at an unknown sound, she lies down in fear, and her pounding heartbeat becomes so intense she worries it will be heard.

The narrator's bodily reactions drive the suspense of the moment.

The other three choices narrate events, family actions, or timelines, but none convey suspense through physical response.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
